text: To resolve Redis downtime during scaling, ensure graceful retries and backup replication. During the last scale-up event, the primary node became unreachable for 90 seconds while new shards were provisioned. Clients without retry logic returned hard errors instead of falling back to replicas.

This document covers failover strategy for clustered systems, including leader election timing, split-brain avoidance, and how downstream services should treat a temporary loss of quorum as retryable rather than fatal.

Rate limiting should be applied at the API gateway using a token bucket algorithm. Clients that exceed their quota receive a 429 with a Retry-After header. Retries should use exponential backoff with jitter to avoid thundering herd effects.

The ingestion service reads documents from an S3 bucket and validates schema before writing to the queue. Malformed records are moved to a dead-letter queue and alerted on via PagerDuty rather than blocking the batch.

Kafka consumer lag spiked during the March incident because a downstream consumer group was stuck reprocessing a poison message. The fix was adding a max-retry count before routing to a dead-letter topic.
This integration guide explains how to authenticate service-to-service calls using short-lived JWTs issued by the internal identity provider, and how to rotate signing keys without downtime.

Database connection pool exhaustion caused cascading timeouts across three dependent services. Root cause was a missing connection timeout on a slow query path, which held pool slots indefinitely under load.

Changelog for v2.3: added support for horizontal pod autoscaling based on custom Redis queue-depth metrics, deprecated the legacy polling-based scaler, and fixed a race condition in leader election on pod restart.
